question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send requests with storage=true for a transaction that wrote to many storage slots, causing the storage trace to enumerate all modified slots and produce a response so large that it causes network buffer exhaustion or client-side memory issues?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user send Accept-Encoding: GZIP (uppercase) and have it pass the toLowerCase().contains('gzip') check, confirming the check is case-insensitive, but then observe whether the actual HTTP response is actually gzip-compressed by the server, potentially exposing a mismatch between the validation and actual compression behavior?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the @RequestHeader for Accept-Encoding has no 'required = false' annotation, meaning Spring throws MissingRequestHeaderException before the controller body executes, and this exception path is not subject to the throttle check, allowing unlimited header-probing requests?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request where the transactionIdOrHash path variable contains special regex characters or null bytes, and does the TransactionIdOrHashParameter.valueOf() error message 'Unsupported ID format: %s' reflect the raw attacker-controlled input back in the response, enabling reflected content injection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint has no authentication requirement and is accessible to any HTTP client, to use it as an oracle for determining whether specific transaction hashes exist on the network by observing 404 vs 200 response codes?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a valid transactionIdOrHash and observe the response time difference between a transaction that exists vs one that does not, using timing side-channels to enumerate valid transaction hashes without triggering rate limits (since invalid-format hashes are rejected before throttling)?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the single-token global opcodeRateLimitBucket to perform a low-and-slow DoS: by sending exactly 1 request per second with a computationally expensive transaction, keeping the server's EVM re-execution thread continuously occupied while staying within the rate limit?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash that is a valid hex hash (64 chars) but with mixed case (e.g., 0xAbCd...), and does the Bytes.fromHexString() parsing handle this consistently, or could case normalization differences cause hash lookup mismatches that expose different data than expected?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash containing a 0x-prefixed hash where the hex portion is exactly 64 characters, and exploit any difference in how the 0x prefix is stripped vs not stripped to cause a hash collision or lookup against a different transaction?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the OpcodeRequest is constructed with the raw TransactionIdOrHashParameter without any additional sanitization or re-validation, and pass a parameter that was valid at parse time but causes unexpected behavior in the downstream OpcodeServiceImpl.buildCallServiceParameters() method?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with stack=false&memory=false&storage=false to minimize the response size while still triggering the full EVM re-execution, effectively using the endpoint as a free EVM computation service without receiving the expensive output?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint re-executes transactions on the EVM to use it as a side-channel to determine the exact gas consumption of historical transactions, potentially revealing information about contract logic that was not publicly disclosed?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a TransactionIdParameter where the validStart nanoseconds field is at maximum (999999999, 9 digits) and the seconds field is at Long.MAX_VALUE, causing convertToNanosMax() to overflow when computing nanoseconds, leading to incorrect timestamp lookup or exception?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of any CORS policy enforcement visible in the controller to make cross-origin browser requests to the opcodes endpoint, potentially enabling a malicious website to exfiltrate opcode traces of transactions on behalf of a victim user's browser?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a valid transactionIdOrHash and observe that the OpcodesResponse includes the contract address and contractId in plaintext, enabling enumeration of contract addresses associated with specific transaction hashes without any authorization?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the opcodes endpoint exposes the full returnValue of the contract call in the OpcodesResponse, potentially revealing sensitive return data from private contract functions that was not intended to be publicly accessible?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash that is a valid transaction ID format (shard.realm.num-seconds-nanos) but with shard=0, realm=0, num=0 (EntityId.EMPTY), and does the system handle this edge case without exposing unexpected data or crashing?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: validateAcceptEncodingHeader()] Can an unprivileged external user send Accept-Encoding: identity, gzip to pass the contains('gzip') check while also requesting identity encoding, causing the server to send an uncompressed response despite the validation passing, negating the bandwidth protection?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the throttle check is a single global in-memory bucket (not distributed/persistent) to bypass rate limiting by sending requests to multiple instances of the service simultaneously, each with their own independent opcodeRateLimitBucket?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash path variable that is exactly at the boundary of Spring's default path variable length limit, and does exceeding this limit cause an unhandled exception that leaks server configuration details?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the OpcodeRequest is created with stack=true by default (defaultValue = 'true') without any user opt-in, meaning every request automatically includes the most expensive trace option unless explicitly disabled, making the default behavior the most resource-intensive?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a valid transactionIdOrHash and observe that the failed field in the OpcodesResponse reveals whether the original transaction succeeded or failed, enabling enumeration of transaction outcomes for any transaction hash without authorization?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the gas field in the OpcodesResponse reveals the exact gas used by the replayed transaction, enabling precise gas profiling of any historical contract execution to reverse-engineer contract logic?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash that maps to a contract creation transaction (CONTRACTCREATEINSTANCE type), and does the EVM re-execution of a contract creation expose the full constructor bytecode and initialization parameters in the opcode trace?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/controller/OpcodesController.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getContractOpcodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a request with a transactionIdOrHash that maps to a transaction involving a precompile contract, and does the opcode trace expose internal precompile execution details that reveal sensitive system-level information?
